chunk_summary: Adolescent growth spurts require tailored nutrient counseling to address rapid growth, bone accrual, and iron needs, with emphasis on calcium, vitamin D, and protein quality. Practical meal swaps and timing strategies are key for effective dietary interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the physiological demands of adolescent growth spurts and their nutritional implications.
Identify key nutrient targets such as calcium, vitamin D, and iron for pubertal counseling.
Develop practical meal-based strategies to improve adolescent dietary adequacy.
Recognize the role of menarche timing in iron requirements and overall nutritional assessment.
key_facts: Adolescents require ~1000 mg of calcium daily during mid-to-late adolescence.
Vitamin D intake should be ~600 IU/day when not deficient.
Iron needs increase significantly after menarche in girls compared to boys.
Protein quality matters; cereal-based diets may require ~1 g/kg/day of protein.
Low energy availability can lead to delayed pubertal progression, menstrual irregularities, and fatigue.
Pair iron-rich meals with vitamin C sources and avoid tea/coffee to enhance absorption.
reference_quote: Use ICMR-NIN nutrient reference values as counseling anchors, but state the qualifiers explicitly: targets assume reference age/sex body weights; requirements vary with pubertal stage, growth velocity, and physical activity.
document_key_topics: Nutritional needs during adolescent growth spurts
Impact of menarche timing on nutrient requirements
Practical dietary counseling strategies for adolescents
